questions.question: Define group and explain the four fundamental properties that a set must satisfy to be called a group.
keyPoints: Group: set with binary operation satisfying closure, associativity, identity, and invertibility
Closure: for all a,b in G, a*b is in G
Associativity: (a*b)*c = a*(b*c) for all a,b,c in G
Identity: exists e in G such that e*a = a*e = a for all a
Invertibility: for each a in G, exists a⁻¹ such that a*a⁻¹ = a⁻¹*a = e

questions.question: State and prove Lagrange's Theorem.
keyPoints: Statement: If H is a subgroup of a finite group G, then |H| divides |G|
Proof uses concept of left cosets
Partition G into disjoint cosets
Each coset has same number of elements as H
Number of cosets = index [G:H] = |G|/|H|
Therefore |H| divides |G|

questions.question: Explain the difference between cyclic group and Abelian group with examples.
keyPoints: Cyclic group: generated by single element, G = ⟨a⟩ = {aⁿ : n∈Z}
Example: Z under addition is cyclic (generated by 1)
Abelian group: operation is commutative, a*b = b*a
Example: (Z, +) is both cyclic and Abelian
Every cyclic group is Abelian but not vice versa
Example of non-cyclic Abelian: Z₂ × Z₄

questions.question: What is a subgroup? State and prove the necessary and sufficient condition for a subset to be a subgroup.
keyPoints: Subgroup: subset of G that forms a group under same operation
Conditions: non-empty, closed under operation, contains inverses
Two-step subgroup test: if H is non-empty and a,b∈H implies ab⁻¹∈H
Then H is a subgroup
Proof: Show closure by substituting b = a⁻¹, get identity in H
Then show inverses exist, and associativity follows from G

questions.question: Define order of an element in a group. Find the order of each element in Z₁₂ under addition modulo 12.
keyPoints: Order of element a: smallest positive n such that aⁿ = e
In Z₁₂: 0 has order 1 (identity)
1 has order 12, 2 has order 6, 3 has order 4
4 has order 3, 5 has order 12, 6 has order 2
7 has order 12, 8 has order 3, 9 has order 4
10 has order 6, 11 has order 12
Orders divide 12 (Lagrange's theorem)

questions.question: Prove that every cyclic group is Abelian. Also show that in a cyclic group, there is exactly one subgroup for each divisor of the group order.
keyPoints: For cyclic group G = ⟨a⟩, any two elements are aᵐ and aⁿ
aᵐ × aⁿ = aᵐ⁺ⁿ = aⁿ × aᵐ = aⁿ × aᵐ (using integer addition)
Since addition of integers is commutative, group is Abelian
Let |G| = n, and d be any divisor of n
There exists unique subgroup of order d: ⟨aⁿ/ᵈ⟩
If d₁ and d₂ are different divisors, subgroups are different
Proof of uniqueness: if ⟨aᵐ⟩ = ⟨aᵏ⟩ then d₁ = d₂

questions.question: Let G be a group. Prove that: (i) Identity element is unique (ii) Each element has a unique inverse (iii) (a⁻¹)⁻¹ = a for any a ∈ G (iv) (ab)⁻¹ = b⁻¹a⁻¹
keyPoints: (i) If e and f are identities: e = e*f = f
(ii) If b and c are inverses of a: b = b*e = b*(a*c) = (b*a)*c = e*c = c
(iii) Show a*(a⁻¹)⁻¹ = e, so (a⁻¹)⁻¹ = a by uniqueness
(iv) Show (ab)(b⁻¹a⁻¹) = a(bb⁻¹)a⁻¹ = aea⁻¹ = aa⁻¹ = e
Hence (ab)⁻¹ = b⁻¹a⁻¹

questions.question: Define: (i) Order of a group (ii) Order of an element (iii) Cyclic group (iv) Generator. Find all generators of group Z₈ under addition.
keyPoints: (i) Order of group = number of elements in G
(ii) Order of element = smallest n>0 such that aⁿ=e
(iii) Cyclic group: generated by single element
(iv) Generator: element whose powers give all group elements
Generators are elements with order 8: 1, 3, 5, 7
Because gcd(1,8)=gcd(3,8)=gcd(5,8)=gcd(7,8)=1
Elements with order 1:0, order 2:4, order 4:2,6
